items.title: Workplace fall to paralysis: Media Prima fund aids bedridden father
items.summary: KLANG: The Media Prima-NSTP Humanitarian Fund today contributed RM5,000 to a bedridden father who was paralysed after suffering a spinal cord injury in a workplace accident. Media Prima Corporate Social Responsibility and Sponsorship manager Muhammad Farris Effendy Mohd Salim said the fund visited 38-year-old Zakri Omar at his home in Bukit Kapar, Meru, and presented him with RM5,000 in immediate financial aid. Farris said Zakri's wife, Nur Athirah Rezaide, 30, had given up her career to become his full-time caregiver.

items.title: MAF confirms Malbatt personnel safe after Lebanon mortar attack kills Spanish UN peacekeeper
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) has confirmed that all personnel of the Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 serving in southern Lebanon are safe and unaffected following a mortar attack on UNP 7-2, the Spanish Battalion (Spanbatt) position in the Eastern Sector, on Wednesday. In a statement today, the MAF said the incident, which occurred at 11.20pm local time, left three Spanbatt peacekeepers injured, while one of them was confirmed dead yesterday morning. “The MAF extends its condolences to the family of the fallen Spanbatt peacekeeper and prays for the swift recovery of those injured,” the statement said.

items.title: Penang water operator's profits insufficient to fund major projects, says Zairil
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang has acknowledged that while the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) remains profitable, its earnings are insufficient to fund major water infrastructure projects. State Infrastructure, Transport and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said tariff adjustments were necessary to ensure adequate funding for critical upgrades and expansion works. Elaborating, Zairil said the state was undertaking multi-billion-ringgit water infrastructure developments to strengthen long-term supply security and prevent disruptions.

items.title: PH, BN contesting separately won't affect unity government, says Fahmi
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Cooperation within the unity government remains strong despite Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) deciding to contest separately in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan elections. Unity Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the state-level decisions by the two coalitions would not affect the federal administration. "There is no impact on the unity government at the federal level.

items.title: Student loses RM16,800 after RM1,000 online loan turns into extortion scam
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A RM1,000 online loan taken by a 19-year-old student spiralled into losses of RM16,800 after he allegedly fell victim to a loan scam involving a series of fabricated charges. MCA Youth deputy chief Mike Chong said the student borrowed RM1,000 from an online lender on May 12 but received only RM700 after deductions. "The victim agreed to repay the loan in two instalments of RM500.

items.title: Noor Hisam moves to Bukit Aman, Mohd Alwi steps up as Perak police chief
items.summary: IPOH, June 5 — Penang deputy police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin has been appointed as the new Perak chief police officer, effective today. The handover of duties ceremony, held at the Senior Police Officers’ Mess in Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah here, was witnessed by Bukit Aman Internal Security and Public Order Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri. Mohd Alwi replaces Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin, who has taken over as director of the Bukit Aman Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department.

items.title: Perikatan’s ‘pluses and minuses’ not my concern, Anwar says amid PAS push to broaden ties
items.summary: PUCHONG, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today dismissed talk of Perikatan Nasional (PN) expanding into a broader political pact, saying he had no interest in tracking the Opposition’s political arithmetic. Anwar said his focus remained on running the government, not on speculation over PN’s attempts to court other Malay-based parties. Anwar was asked to comment on PN’s reported proposal to form “PN Plus” by bringing in other Malay-based parties.

items.title: High Court grants Najib partial access to 1MDB documents in US$8b suit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The High Court here today partially allowed an application by former Prime Minister Datuk Seri Najib Tun Razak to obtain several documents ahead of the hearing of the US$8 billion civil suit filed by 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) and its subsidiaries against him and seven others. Judicial Commissioner Mohamad Redzuan Idrus granted Najib access to five categories of documents, including a complete list of all monies, assets and properties recovered, returned, repatriated or realised, as well as documents detailing the terms and conditions of settlements relating to 1MDB’s financial losses arising from alleged abuse of power and breaches of fiduciary duties. The suit, filed on May 7, 2021, initially named 15 individuals, including Najib, as defendants.

items.title: 'I couldn't keep living in fear': Entrepreneur loses RM400,000 to scam syndicate
items.summary: Bryan Teow, an education entrepreneur, said a RM20,000 loan application he made after seeing a Facebook advertisement in August 2024 led to almost 20 months of intimidation, threats and financial losses. Facing cash flow difficulties at the time, Teow said he trusted the individual and submitted the requested documents. Teow said the scammer repeatedly changed their demands and used threats to pressure him into making payments.

items.title: Northern Gulf contract win keeps Velesto's Naga 6 busy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A new contract from Northern Gulf Petroleum is expected to keep Velesto Energy Bhd's Naga 6 rig deployed after its existing charter with Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd ended in March, a research house said. Velesto's subsidiary Velesto Sumber Sdn Bhd recently received a letter of award (LoA) from Northern Gulf for the provision of the Naga 6 jack-up rig and related services. Although the contract value was not disclosed, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said the scope comprises four infill wells and three exploration wells, with optional one plus one exploration wells.

items.title: Bukit Aman investigating claim Malaysian woman set up by daughter in Nigeria cannabis arrest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Police are investigating claims that a 63-year-old woman detained by Nigerian authorities for allegedly attempting to smuggle 31 kilogrammes (kg) of Canadian Loud cannabis into the country had been set up by her daughter. Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the claim is under investigation and efforts to trace the woman’s daughter are ongoing. International media previously reported that the woman was detained by Nigerian authorities on May 17 after allegedly attempting to smuggle 31kg of Canadian Loud cannabis into the country through Murtala Muhammed International Airport in Lagos.

items.title: Tuanku Muhriz is still Negeri Sembilan Ruler, Anwar says despite Melaka hotel proclamation
items.summary: PUCHONG, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reaffirmed Putrajaya’s recognition of Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir as the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, as the state’s royal succession dispute widened into the courts, the police and now a snap election. “Based on the constitutional line of succession as it stands, we continue to recognise Tuanku Muhriz as the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan. Anwar said while individuals have the right to seek remedies under the law and state constitution, attempts to remove a ruler carry far-reaching implications for the country.

items.title: Malaysian Unifil patrols suspended after mortar attack kills Spanish peacekeeper
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian peacekeepers under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) have been ordered to temporarily suspend patrols following a mortar attack that killed a Spanish peacekeeper. The Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) said patrols had been halted immediately and security measures raised to Status Level Red (the highest level) as part of enhanced force protection measures after the attack on the UNP 7-2 Spanish Battalion (Spanbatt) area in the Eastern Sector on Wednesday. The mortar attack injured three Spanbatt peacekeepers.

items.title: Police officer found dead at home
items.summary: SIBU: A police officer was found dead at his home in Jalan Kampung Nangka here yesterday. The 45-year-old assistant superintendent was found unconscious in the master bedroom by his wife, who alerted police at 8.30pm. Sibu police chief Assistant Commissioner Azari Miskon said a team from the Sungai Merah police station and the Sibu Criminal Investigation Department was despatched to the scene.

items.title: Muhyiddin not in Johor state poll plans, says state Perikatan chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin will not be making a return to contest in the upcoming Johor election, according to Johor Perikatan Nasional (PN) chief Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal. The confirmation comes amid mounting speculation over candidate line-ups after Johor's state assembly was dissolved this week ahead of fresh polls. He said there had been no discussions within Johor PN regarding the former Gambir assemblyman contesting any state seat.

items.title: Anwar backs Negeri Sembilan assembly dissolution, says Johor polls came sooner than hoped
items.summary: PUCHONG, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said he would have preferred Johor to delay its state election by a few months to allow the government to focus on economic recovery and addressing people’s concerns. The Johor State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 1, paving the way for the state election to be held within 60 days. In the 2022 Johor state election, Barisan Nasional won 40 seats, PH secured 12, Perikatan Nasional won three, while Muda captured one.

items.title: Taxi driver claims trial to trafficking 748g of cannabis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 55-year-old taxi driver pleaded not guilty in the High Court today to trafficking 748.59g of cannabis last year. Mohd Faris Ali is accused of committing the offence at 5pm near a madrasah along Jalan 3/149J in Bandar Baru Seri Petaling on Jan 23, 2025. The charge, framed under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and punishable under Section 39B(2), carries the death penalty or life imprisonment upon conviction.

items.title: Transparency International Malaysia warns against ‘pay-to-settle’ perception in graft cases, calls for clearer oversight
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Transparency International Malaysia (TI-M) today expressed concern over the increasing use of compounds, settlements and charge withdrawals in corruption-related cases, warning that repeated reliance on such mechanisms without adequate public explanation risks weakening confidence in anti-corruption institutions and perceptions of accountability. While acknowledging that Malaysian law permits compounds and other discretionary mechanisms in certain circumstances, TI-M said there must be greater transparency, consistency and public confidence in how such powers are exercised. “While asset recovery serves an important public interest objective, recovery alone should not be regarded as a substitute for accountability,” TI-M president Raymon Ram said in a statement.

items.title: Malaysia denounces Israel’s violations in Lebanon, urges protection of UN peacekeepers and civilians
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Malaysia has condemned the continuing attacks on United Nations peacekeepers in Lebanon and extended its condolences to Serbia following the death of a Serbian peacekeeper serving with the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil). In a statement on Friday, the Foreign Ministry said Malaysia stood in solidarity with Unifil and condemned in the strongest terms the continuing attacks targeting United Nations peacekeepers. Malaysia is among the countries contributing personnel to Unifil, which operates in southern Lebanon under a United Nations mandate.

items.title: Nada raids Terengganu drug transit hub, 196 addicts detained including two civil servants
items.summary: DUNGUN, June 5 — A total of 196 drug addicts, including three women, were detained in an operation by the National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada) in Terengganu. Nada Enforcement and Security Division director Mahadzir Elias said the operation was conducted in the districts of Kuala Terengganu, Hulu Terengganu, Besut and Dungun from Wednesday till today, adding that two civil servants were also detained. No one was harmed in the incident, Mahadzir said.

items.title: Court allows Najib partial access to documents in 1MDB's US$8 billion lawsuit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Najib Razak has been granted access to key documents for the 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) lawsuit seeking US$8 billion in damages from him and seven others. Judicial Commissioner Mohamad Redzuan Idrus allowed the former prime minister's discovery application to inspect five documents ahead of the trial in August. Plaintiffs 1MDB and its four subsidiaries - 1MDB Energy Holdings Limited, 1MDB Energy Limited, 1MDB Energy Limited, and Global Diversified Investment Company Limited - named Najib as the first defendant.

items.title: MCMC probes The Coverage Media content after public complaints
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is investigating content linked to Facebook page The Coverage Media following public complaints. In a statement today, MCMC said its preliminary assessment found several posts believed to be in breach of Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998. MCMC's response came after Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching called on the commission to provide an immediate explanation over the restriction imposed on The Coverage Media's Facebook page.

items.title: GIIB says unaware of new trigger behind share surge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: GIIB Holdings says it is unaware of any undisclosed developments or market rumours that could explain the recent spike in its share price and trading activity. In response to Bursa Malaysia's query, the company said discussions on a previously disclosed potential investment in a healthcare-related business remain ongoing, with terms yet to be finalised. GIIB said there are no new corporate developments behind the unusual trading activity, apart from an earlier announced healthcare investment proposal still under negotiation.

items.title: Consumer credit authority sets rules for non-bank credit players
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Consumer Credit Commission (CCC) has issued licensing and conduct standards for non-bank credit providers and service providers ahead of the Consumer Credit Act 2025 coming into force. In a statement, CCC said the Authorisation Standards outline the requirements and processes for registration and licensing of authorised entities. CCC executive chairman Abu Hassan Alshari Yahaya said the standards represent the first foundational framework under the Consumer Credit Act 2025, providing clear expectations for market entry and conduct.

items.title: [UPDATED] PKR meeting focused on Johor, Negri poll preparations, says Anwar
items.summary: PUCHONG: The Johor election and the possible dissolution of the Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly were among the key issues discussed at the PKR Political Bureau meeting last night. PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the meeting also focused on strengthening the party's machinery ahead of upcoming elections. However, he did not disclose whether PKR had finalised seat allocations for the Johor and Negri Sembilan state polls.

items.title: Taxi driver claims trial to cannabis trafficking charge in KL High Court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — A taxi driver pleaded not guilty in the High Court here today to a charge of trafficking 748.59 grammes of cannabis last year. Mohd Faris Ali, 55, entered the plea after the charge was read out to him before Judge Datuk Aslam Zainuddin. He is accused of trafficking the drug at Jalan 3/149J, Bandar Baru Seri Petaling, Kuala Lumpur, at about 5 pm on Jan 23, 2025.

items.title: Blocked in Tampin, proclaimed in Melaka: Rais Yatim says Negeri Sembilan has a new Ruler
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Former Dewan Negara president Tan Sri Rais Yatim today claimed that Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja’afar had been officially proclaimed the 12th Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan in Melaka, in apparent defiance of an interim injunction from the High Court. The proclamation was held at a five-star hotel in Alor Gajah, The Star reported this afternoon, hours after police briefly blocked several Undang and adat leaders from entering the official residence of the Tunku Besar of Tampin, where a special sitting of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) was held. He said Undang Jelebu Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad and Tunku Besar Tampin Tunku Syed Razman Tunku Syed Idrus Al-Qadri were physically present, while three other adat leaders followed the proceedings remotely via Zoom.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar backs Negri Sembilan assembly dissolution to end political uncertainty
items.summary: PUCHONG: A snap election in Negri Sembilan is the best way to resolve the constitutional issue and political uncertainty in the state, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. In backing the state government's decision to seek a dissolution of the state assembly, triggering polls, Anwar said the situation has led to confusion and concern over processes linked to the monarchy. Yesterday, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced that the state assembly would be dissolved today to pave the way for a state election, which must be held within 60 days.

items.title: Penang water tariff revision vital to fund new treatment plants and pipeline upgrades, says exco
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 5 — The implementation of the new water tariff in Penang, scheduled to take effect on July 1, cannot be postponed as it is crucial to ensure that various development and infrastructure upgrading projects can be carried out to safeguard the sustainability of future water supply. State Transport, Infrastructure and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said the profits currently recorded by the Perbadanan Bekalan Air Pulau Pinang (PBAPP) are insufficient to finance the large-scale projects planned to meet rising water demand. “Each water treatment plant requires investments amounting to hundreds of millions of ringgit.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Tuanku Muhriz still recognised as Negri Sembilan ruler
items.summary: PUCHONG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reaffirmed that Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir remains the recognised Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan. Anwar said that any challenge to the position of a ruler must be pursued through the proper legal and constitutional channels. Today's DKU sitting was expected to involve the four Undang, Tunku Besar Tampin Tunku Syed Razman Tunku Syed Idrus Al-Qadri and Tunku Nadzaruddin.

items.title: Clip of Tunku Nadzaruddin being 'proclaimed' Yang di-Pertuan Besar goes viral [WATCH]
items.summary: "In accordance with Article 11 of the Constitution of Negeri Sembilan 1959, we, the Undang Yang Empat, with the powers vested in us, hereby elect and proclaim Tunku Panglima Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja'afar, as the 12th Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, with immediate effect. The "proclamation" was subsequently acknowledged by Tunku Nadzaruddin. Today's DKU sitting was expected to involve the four Undang, Tunku Besar Tampin Tunku Syed Razman Tunku Syed Idrus Al-Qadri and Tunku Nadzaruddin.

items.title: Australia seizes 100,000 cockroaches in bug-breeder bust
items.summary: More than 100,000 contraband cockroaches were found in a raid on a commercial breeder in the town of Bathurst, west of Sydney, Australia's environment department said on Friday. They found Madagascar "hissing" cockroaches, a bulky insect named for its noisy defence mechanism, and dubia cockroaches, an invasive critter bred as a snack for pet lizards. "We take our job protecting Australia's unique biodiversity and breaches of national environment law very seriously," an environment department spokesman said.

items.title: U Mobile's potentially lower-cost 5G push may rattle Malaysia's mobile market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's mobile sector may come under greater competitive pressure once U Mobile launches its potentially lower-cost 5G network and seeks to build market share. "Thus far in Malaysia, we have seen three new MVNOs launched since 2025, with two of these on U Mobile's network. The firm prefers Telekom Malaysia Bhd and Axiata Group Bhd over the mobile players.

items.title: RM66mil boost for Felda staff housing plan [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) has allocated around RM66 million to develop 189 staff housing units in phases under a nationwide modernisation initiative. Felda chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek said following decades of extensive rural transformation, around 90 per cent of Felda settlers now live in modern brick bungalow-style homes. Encorp will develop the 189 units in three phases, comprising 62 units in the first phase, 63 units in the second phase and 64 units in the third phase.

items.title: Sarawak police looking into possible negligence after nine‑year‑old boy drowns at Sematan resort pool
items.summary: See Hua Daily News reported that Civil Defence Force personnel arrived at the scene shortly after receiving the report at 5.38pm and administered cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) on the boy. However, the child was later pronounced dead at the scene despite efforts to revive him. Lundu district police chief Supt Sudirman Kram, when contacted, confirmed the incident.

items.title: Abdul Karim says Sarawak polls must be held this year but timing remains Premier’s prerogative amid Dr Sim’s 100‑day remark
items.summary: KUCHING, June 5 — The timing of exactly when the next Sarawak state election will be held remains the prerogative of Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Openg, said Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu (PBB) information chief Datuk Seri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah. Commenting on Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian’s remark that the election may be called within the next 100 days, Abdul Karim said the statement was Dr Sim’s personal assessment based on the current political landscape. He said all political parties are aware that the election must be held this year as the current State Legislative Assembly term expires by early next year.

items.title: Foreigner arrested for making obscene gesture, leering at woman at cafe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a foreigner for indecent behaviour and making obscene gestures towards a woman at a coffee outlet in Brickfields. Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said the 32-year-old man was arrested following a report lodged by a woman who claimed she was harassed while at a roadside eatery along Jalan Tun Sambanthan on Wednesday around 10pm. "The complainant and her friend were walking towards a coffee shop located at Jalan Tun Sambanthan, Brickfields.

items.title: Victims’ family mulls civil suit after losing four members in Simpang Renggam crash
items.summary: DUNGUN, June 5 — The family of four victims killed in a crash at KM27 of Jalan Renggam-Simpang Renggam last Monday is considering filing a civil suit seeking compensation from the individual believed to have caused the accident. Fauziah Abdullah, 59, said the move was aimed at seeking justice for all the victims — her son, Mohd Aiman Mohd Rashid, 36; daughter-in-law, Nor Azlina Latif, 33; granddaughter, Nur Airish Syifa Sidek, 10; and Nor Azlina’s mother, Semek Mat Soh, 73. Meanwhile, Nur Airish Syifa’s father, Sidek Abd Hamid, 42, said he has yet to consult lawyers on pursuing a civil suit against those allegedly responsible for the crash.

items.title: Speaker: Negeri Sembilan assembly dissolution notice to reach Election Commission today
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 5 — An official notice on the dissolution of the Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly will be submitted to the Election Commission (EC) today. Yesterday, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced that the state assembly would be dissolved effective June 5 to pave the way for a state election, which must be held within 60 days. In the 2023 state election, the Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) alliance formed the Negeri Sembilan government after securing 31 of the 36 seats in the assembly.

items.title: Negri Sembilan assembly dissolution notice to be submitted to EC today
items.summary: SEREMBAN: An official notice on the dissolution of the Negri Sembilan assembly will be submitted to the Election Commission (EC) today. Yesterday, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced that the state assembly will be dissolved today to pave the way for a state election, which must be held within 60 days. In the 2023 state election, the Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) alliance formed the Negri Sembilan government after securing 31 of the 36 seats in the assembly.

items.title: Philippine annual inflation slows to 6.8pct in May
items.summary: MANILA: Philippine annual inflation stood at 6.8 per cent in May, below the previous month's 7.2 per cent rate, driven mainly by slower increases in food and transport costs, the statistics agency said on Friday. It was also below the central bank's projection that annual inflation in May would be within a 7.1 per cent to 7.9 per cent range. The average inflation rate in the five months to May was 4.5 per cent, above the central bank's 2.0 per cent to 4.0 per cent target.

items.title: Indonesia issues regulation to bring strategic commodity exports under central control
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia issued a much-anticipated regulation on Friday to bring exports of its strategic commodities under central government control, a move aimed at boosting state earnings and stabilisingits rupiah currency. President Prabowo Subianto announced on May 20 that Indonesia would bring exports of all of its strategic commodities under the control of a new state company, a move that has spooked investors. "In the export of strategic natural resource commodities by the state-owned export enterprise ... the selling price shall be determined by the state-owned export enterprise," it said, adding that the enterprise can also set margins.

items.title: Seremban High Court halts Negri Sembilan DKU meeting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court in Seremban granted an ad interim injunction to halt a planned special sitting of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang Negeri Sembilan (DKU) and block any move to remove its secretary, Raja Norazli Raja Nordin, pending the disposal of legal challenges before the court. Judge Roz Mawar Rozain granted the injunctions sought by Raja Norazli and the DKU to preserve the status quo of the body's administration. Yesterday, Negri Sembilan State Secretary Datuk Mohd Zafir Ibrahim said Raja Norazli's appointment as DKU secretary was made by Tuanku Muhriz in accordance with the state constitution.

items.title: Dr Sim says next Sarawak state election could be called within 100 days
items.summary: KUCHING, June 5 — Sarawak United Peoples’ Party (SUPP) president Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian said the next state election is around the corner and may be called within the next 100 days. He said they must realise that the forthcoming election is a battle that requires thorough preparation and a full commitment from all members. Stressing that the next state election “is not far off”, he said party members must play their part to speak out and fight for Sarawak.

items.title: Taylor’s University campus evacuated after ‘security incident’ in Subang Jaya
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 5 — Taylor’s University’s campus in Subang Jaya was evacuated today following what the institution described as a “security incident”. FMT reported that police in Subang Jaya have been contacted for comment. The incident comes a day after Monash University’s campus in Subang Jaya was evacuated over an alleged bomb threat.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends morning session higher
items.summary: Johor Plantations Group Bhd advanced three sen to RM1.73. Heavyweight counters Malayan Banking Bhd added 18 sen to RM10.78, Public Bank Bhd and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd expanded nine sen each to RM4.87 and RM7.44, respectively. Tenaga Nasional Bhd gained eight sen to RM14.18, and IHH Healthcare Bhd increased two sen to RM8.81.

items.title: Dewan Rakyat sitting to proceed on June 22 as scheduled, says Speaker’s Office
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The Second Meeting of the Fifth Session of the 15th Parliament, scheduled to begin on June 22, will proceed according to the set calendar, according to the Office of Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul. In confirming the matter to Bernama today, it said that there are no changes to the Dewan Rakyat sitting schedule. The first meeting of the current Dewan Rakyat session ended on March 3 after a 20-day sitting.

items.title: Muhyiddin to sit out Johor polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin will not contest the upcoming Johor state election. "So far no, and I have not been informed (about Muhyiddin contesting a state seat)," Dr Sahruddin said when contacted. Dr Sahruddin previously said seat negotiations among state PN component parties were nearly finalised.

items.title: MCA confirms contest in Johor state election, seat talks ongoing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong has confirmed the Barisan Nasional (BN) component party will contest in the upcoming 16th Johor state election (PRN16). Wee, who is also Ayer Hitam MP, told Berita Harian that the number of seats MCA will contest is still under negotiation. “Why wouldn’t MCA contest?

items.title: Cops reportedly blocked Tunku Nadzaruddin from attending Negri Sembilan Undang meeting
items.summary: TAMPIN: Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Jaafar, who was scheduled to attend a Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) meeting at the Tunku Besar Tampin's residence, is believed to have been stopped by police from leaving a hotel in Melaka. Among those involved were Undang Luak Johol Datuk Muhammed Abdullah, Undang Luak Jelebu Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad, Undang Luak Rembau Datuk Abd Rahim Yassin and Datuk Syahbandar Sungei Ujong Datuk Seri Utama Badarudin Abdul Khalid. Also absent were Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin Tuanku Muhriz and Undang Luak Sungei Ujong Datuk Mubarak Dohak, who removed from office.

items.title: Police: No criminal wrongdoing in alleged sexual assault at Melaka mosque mortuary
items.summary: MELAKA, June 5 — Police investigations into the case of alleged molestation and rape in a mosque mortuary in Melaka found no elements of criminal wrongdoing. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the case has now been referred to the Melaka Islamic Religious Department for further action under the Melaka Syariah Offences Enactment 1991. He said a thorough investigation, however, found that the alleged criminal acts did not take place.

items.title: Nvidia CEO sees robotics as next major sector in South Korea
items.summary: SOUTH KOREA: Nvidia chief executive officer Jensen Huang said on Friday that he sees robotics as the next major sector in South Korea. He was talking to reporters after arriving at Gimpo airport in South Korea on a flight from Taiwan. "Did I bring any gifts for Korea?

items.title: Negeri Sembilan DKU seeks court injunction to block chiefs from meeting, ousting secretary
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 5 — The Dewan Keadilan dan Undang Negeri Sembilan (DKU) has applied for an interlocutory injunction to stop several ruling chiefs from holding a special sitting and from removing its secretary, Raja Norazli Raja Nordin. The application, filed in the High Court here, seeks to restrain the plaintiffs — five Undang and one Tunku Besar — from convening any DKU meetings, including the special sitting announced for June 5, and from interfering with Raja Norazli’s role. The DKU also asked the court to bar the chiefs from setting up committees to assume the secretary’s powers, issuing statements on behalf of the council, or making decisions that alter its governance and affairs.

items.title: Teen driver in Simpang Renggam crash remanded four days for murder probe
items.summary: KLUANG, June 5 — The 19-year-old driver involved in a fatal crash that claimed five lives in Simpang Renggam near Kluang on June 1 was remanded again today to facilitate investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder. The suspect had previously been released on police bail after being remanded for three days until yesterday to assist investigations under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987. However, police rearrested the suspect to enable investigations to be conducted under Section 302 of the Penal Code.

items.title: PM orders MOHE, MOH to expedite resolution of Alexandria PharmD recognition issue for affected students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed the Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) and the Ministry of Health (MOH) to quickly resolve the issue of recognition for the Doctor of Pharmacy (PharmD) qualification awarded to graduates of Alexandria University. According to the statement, the PMO has taken note of complaints raised by pharmacy students at the university regarding the recognition of the PharmD qualification for registration and professional practice purposes in Malaysia. However, professional recognition for registration as a pharmacist requires consideration and approval by the Pharmacy Board Malaysia.

items.title: High Court halts Negeri Sembilan DKU meeting, bars chiefs from removing secretary Raja Norazli
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 5 — The High Court today stepped in to halt a planned Dewan Keadilan dan Undang Negeri Sembilan (DKU) meeting and blocked attempts to remove its secretary, Raja Norazli Raja Nordin, until the case is heard in full. Judge Roz Mawar Rozain granted temporary injunctions after Raja Norazli and the DKU applied to preserve the status quo of the body’s administration. With the ruling, the plaintiffs are prevented from removing Raja Norazli, conducting DKU affairs, or issuing press statements on DKU matters.

items.title: Anwar to launch Johor Pakatan election machinery tomorrow ahead of state polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, is scheduled to launch the Johor PH election machinery for the 16th Johor state election tomorrow night. “Launch of Johor PH’s state election machinery. In the 2022 Johor state election, Barisan Nasional won 40 seats, PH secured 12, Perikatan Nasional won three, while MUDA captured one.

items.title: Sabah study shows stateless children excluded from healthcare, schooling and social protection systems
items.summary: The Sabah Child Wellbeing Index (SCWI) 2026 — a large-scale survey of 4,441 children across the state — found that stateless and undocumented children recorded the lowest outcomes in health, education, nutrition, and development. The two-phase study which purposefully included 591 children identified as stateless or undocumented (stateless), while a smaller number of 59 children were identified as undocumented without being classified as stateless. In terms of health, while 92.3 per cent of all children in Sabah reported access to healthcare, only 0.6 per cent of undocumented or stateless children met the overall health wellbeing threshold.

items.title: Sabah survey finds major gaps in child wellbeing despite near-universal healthcare access
items.summary: The index found that only 55.9 per cent of children met the overall health wellbeing benchmark, despite 92.3 per cent having access to healthcare services. Health wellbeing gap despite high access to healthcare services The SCWI measures whether children meet minimum standards across indicators including immunisation, nutrition and age-specific health outcomes. Health outcomes were strongest among children aged five and below, but declined sharply among older age groups, with only 20.8 per cent of adolescents aged 13 to 17 meeting the health benchmark.
